rationale: Simulates real-time interactive inference (chatbot/assistant use case)
impact_on_results: Isolates per-request latency; batch>1 would improve throughput but not user-perceived TTFT

rationale: Native Apple Silicon GPU backend — faster than CPU, avoids PCIe overhead
impact_on_results: Results are specific to Apple unified-memory architecture; not directly comparable to CUDA

rationale: Standard inference precision; balances accuracy and memory footprint
impact_on_results: float32 would double memory bandwidth demand; bfloat16 not supported on MPS

rationale: Fits comfortably in 8GB RAM; preserves transformer inference behavior for latency analysis
impact_on_results: Smaller model means lower absolute latency; trends generalize but absolute values do not

rationale: Allows MPS kernel compilation and cache population before measurement
impact_on_results: Without warmup, first run TTFT is 2–3x higher (see 06_cold_vs_warm_run.csv)

rationale: Sufficient for mean+stdev estimation; balances measurement time vs statistical validity
impact_on_results: p90/p99 estimates from 05 trials are approximate; 50-trial distribution in 10_latency_variance.csv

value: Repeated fixed text, truncated to target token count
rationale: Controlled input isolates prompt-length effect from content-dependent behavior
impact_on_results: Real prompts with varied structure may show ±10–15% additional variance

rationale: Covers short (code snippet) to long (document) generation scenarios
impact_on_results: Longer outputs increase E2E latency linearly; PTL itself stays roughly constant

rationale: Required for realistic decode performance; disabling would make PTL constant but unrealistic
impact_on_results: KV-cache enabled means PTL grows with context — this is the key finding of the study

rationale: perf_counter gives nanosecond resolution; MPS sync ensures GPU work completes before stop
impact_on_results: Without sync(), measured times underestimate true latency (async execution)
